m	Z

Z!d'd

annotationsN)
TYPE_CHECKING
Collection
Iterator
Sequence
Union
cast)
is_list_like)
Colormap
default
intz
Colormap | None
strz3dict[str, Color] | Color | Collection[Color] | None)
num_colors
colormap
color_type
colorc
    Get standard colors based on `colormap`, `color_type` or `color` inputs.
    Parameters
    ----------
    num_colors : int
        Minimum number of colors to be returned.
        Ignored if `color` is a dictionary.
    colormap : :py:class:`matplotlib.colors.Colormap`, optional
        Matplotlib colormap.
        When provided, the resulting colors will be derived from the colormap.
    color_type : {"default", "random"}, optional
        Type of colors to derive. Used if provided `color` and `colormap` are None.
        Ignored if either `color` or `colormap` are not None.
    color : dict or str or sequence, optional
        Color(s) to be used for deriving sequence of colors.
        Can be either be a dictionary, or a single color (single color string,
        or sequence of floats representing a single color),
        or a sequence of colors.
    Returns
    -------
    dict or list
        Standard colors. Can either be a mapping if `color` was a dictionary,
        or a list of colors with a length of `num_colors` or more.
    Warns
    -----
    UserWarning
        If both `colormap` and `color` are provided.
        Parameter `color` will override.
    
isinstance
dict
_derive_colors
list
_cycle_colors)
colors
G/usr/lib64/python3.9/site-packages/pandas/plotting/_matplotlib/style.py
get_standard_colors
z Color | Collection[Color] | Nonez
str | Colormap | Nonez
list[Color])
returnc
    Derive colors from either `colormap`, `color_type` or `color` inputs.
    Get a list of colors either from `colormap`, or from `color`,
    or from `color_type` (if both `colormap` and `color` are None).
    Parameters
    ----------
    color : str or sequence, optional
        Color(s) to be used for deriving sequence of colors.
        Can be either be a single color (single color string, or sequence of floats
        representing a single color), or a sequence of colors.
    colormap : :py:class:`matplotlib.colors.Colormap`, optional
        Matplotlib colormap.
        When provided, the resulting colors will be derived from the colormap.
    color_type : {"default", "random"}, optional
        Type of colors to derive. Used if provided `color` and `colormap` are None.
        Ignored if either `color` or `colormap`` are not None.
    num_colors : int
        Number of colors to be extracted.
    Returns
    -------
    list
        List of colors extracted.
    Warns
    -----
    UserWarning
        If both `colormap` and `color` are provided.
        Parameter `color` will override.
    Nr
zC'color' and 'colormap' cannot be used simultaneously. Using 'color')
_get_colors_from_colormap
warnings
warn
_get_colors_from_color
_get_colors_from_color_typer
Iterator[Color])
Cycle colors until achieving max of `num_colors` or length of `colors`.
    Extra colors will be ignored by matplotlib if there are more colors
    than needed and nothing needs to be done here.
    N)
	itertools
islice
cycle)
max_colorsr

 is not recognized)

cmapr
Color | Collection[Color])
z!Get colors from user input color.r
Invalid color argument: )
_is_single_colorr
Colorr
!_gen_list_of_colors_from_iterable
boolc
Check if `color` is a single color, not a sequence of colors.
    Single color is of these kinds:
        - Named color "red", "C0", "firebrick"
        - Alias "g"
        - Sequence of floats, such as (0.1, 0.2, 0.3) or (0.1, 0.2, 0.3, 0.4).
    See Also
    --------
    _is_single_string_color
    TF)
_is_single_string_color
_is_floats_colorr6
Collection[Color]c
    Yield colors from string of several letters or from collection of colors.
    z
Invalid color N)
zACheck if color comprises a sequence of floats representing color.
float)
	<genexpr>
z#_is_floats_color.<locals>.<genexpr>)
allr6
z&Get colors from user input color type.r
randomz/color_type must be either 'default' or 'random'N)
_get_default_colors
_get_random_colorsr2
z=Get `num_colors` of default colors from matplotlib rc params.r
z'_get_default_colors.<locals>.<listcomp>z
axes.prop_cycle)
matplotlib.pyplotZ
pyplotZ
rcParams)
pltr
z"Get `num_colors` of random colors.c
_random_colorr(
z&_get_random_colors.<locals>.<listcomp>)
ranger
list[float])
columnr
z4Get a random color represented as a list of length 3r;
comZ
random_stateZ
rand
tolist)
Check if `color` is a single string color.
    Examples of single string colors:
        - 'r'
        - 'g'
        - 'red'
        - 'green'
        - 'C3'
        - 'firebrick'
    Parameters
    ----------
    color : Color
        Color string or sequence of floats.
    Returns
    -------
    bool
        True if `color` looks like a valid color.
        False otherwise.
    FTN)
